old_claim.note: Portable Balance Scales provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Fishing-net sinkers control a net in water and do not derive from or require weighing scales.

source_shape.source_locator: Shell-mound section: reports about 300 fishing-net sinkers with fish bones and dates the two relevant shell mounds to 2860-2280 and 2860-2400 BCE.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Korean Neolithic shell mounds dated from 2860 BCE contain hundreds of fishing-net sinkers and fish remains.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scoped evidence establishes the milestone without the removed dependency. Fishing-net sinkers control a net in water and do not derive from or require weighing scales.

ontology_before: net_sinker_weights treated portable_scales as a direct causal prerequisite.
ontology_after: net_sinker_weights is scoped to the directly evidenced mechanism and no longer claims portable_scales as a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: net_sinker_weights no longer directly depends on portable_scales.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that every implementation within the current net_sinker_weights scope technically requires portable_scales.
short_reason: Fishing-net sinkers control a net in water and do not derive from or require weighing scales.
